text: 2-Methyl-4-nitroaniline (MNA), chemical formula C7H8N2O2, molecular weight 152.15, is a single-component organic molecular crystal exhibiting strong piezoelectric and nonlinear optical properties. It crystallizes in the non-centrosymmetric, polar monoclinic space group Ia (No. 9, alternative setting of Cc), with unit cell a = 7.611 Å, b = 11.630 Å, c = 8.229 Å, β = 94.05°, Z = 4. The structure features discrete MNA molecules held together by intermolecular hydrogen bonding interactions; the non-centrosymmetric polar architecture is compatible with piezoelectricity. Molecules exhibit an electron donor-acceptor character, with charge transfer of about 0.3e between the amino NH2 donor group and the nitro NO2 acceptor group inducing a dipole moment of 6.2 D (observed), with the dipole moment closely aligned along the N-N axis. The crystal structure (CCDC 162581, CSD refcode BAJCIY01) was determined by single-crystal X-ray diffraction at 150 K with an R factor of 4.3% and a calculated density of 1.391 g/cm³. Experimental X-ray diffraction studies using the Barsch equation and three-step modulation method revealed an exceptional piezoelectric tensor dominated by one coefficient: d₁₁ = 13.8(1) × 10⁻¹² C/N, making it the largest ever observed in an organic molecular crystal at the time—six times greater than the largest coefficient of quartz and comparable to strong inorganic piezoelectrics.
experimental_method: X-ray diffraction measurement of piezoelectric tensor using Barsch equation and three-step modulation method
structure_ref_journal: Acta Crystallographica Section C: Crystal Structure Communications
property_ref_title: Measurement of the piezoelectric tensor of an organic crystal by the x-ray method: The nonlinear optical crystal 2-methyl 4-nitroaniline

molecular_properties.donor_acceptor_character: electron donor (NH2) to acceptor (NO2) charge transfer ~0.3e
molecular_properties.dipole_alignment: closely aligned with N-N axis (component 8.64 D)

piezoelectric_tensor_results.measurement_method: X-ray Bragg angle shifts via Barsch equation

crystal_structure_features.layer_structure: molecules arranged in layers perpendicular to E2 direction
crystal_structure_features.hydrogen_bonding_pattern: head-to-tail between NH2 and NO2 groups along E1

nonlinear_optical_properties.second_harmonic_generation: one of the largest known SHG coefficients
nonlinear_optical_properties.applications_limitation: thin films only, due to absence of phase-matching angle in bulk crystals

comparison_with_other_materials.vs_quartz: six times larger than largest quartz coefficient (d33 = 2.3 pC/N)
comparison_with_other_materials.status: largest piezoelectric behavior observed in organic molecular crystal at time of measurement

cartesian_coordinate_system.E1_definition: parallel to 2a+c; polar axis of crystal
cartesian_coordinate_system.E2_definition: parallel to b; perpendicular to mirror plane
cartesian_coordinate_system.E3_definition: parallel to -a*+2c*; in mirror plane, perpendicular to molecular plane of layers

dominated_coefficients_summary.main_effect: tensile strain along polar axis (E1) induced by field along E1
dominated_coefficients_summary.secondary_effect: shear strain e23 perpendicular to polar axis, induced by field along b (E2)
dominated_coefficients_summary.negligible_effect: field along E3 (perpendicular to molecular plane) has no significant effect

electro_optic_interpretation.linear_electro_optic_effect: reinterpreted in terms of piezoelectric-photoelastic effect
electro_optic_interpretation.phonon_electron_coupling: now recognized as important due to large piezoelectric coefficients
electro_optic_interpretation.comparison_analogue: 2-methyl-4-nitro-N-methylaniline (MNMA) shows LEO coefficients dominated by electrically-induced strains

additional_notes: Monoclinic space group Ia (No. 9) is equivalent to Cc (No. 9), alternative setting
BAJCIY01 is a refined redetermination of the same polymorph as BAJCIY
R factor (all data) = 5.6%, R factor (gt) = 4.3%, wR factor = 10.6%
Crystal structure redetermined 2001 by Ferguson et al.; original piezoelectric study 1991 by Paturle et al.
Single-component organic molecular crystal with discrete molecules held by hydrogen bonding
Electron donor-acceptor (D-A) character with charge transfer ~0.3e between NH2 donor and NO2 acceptor
Non-centrosymmetric polar structure satisfies crystallographic requirement for piezoelectricity
Largest piezoelectric coefficient d11 = 13.8 pC/N for organic molecular crystal (at time of study)
X-ray diffraction method using three-step modulation allows measurement of strains as small as 10^-5
Piezoelectric tensor elements refined to high precision (R factor ~5-6%)
Strains induced by piezoelectric effect (~10^-5 for 10^6 V/m fields) lead to cell dimension changes of 10^-4 to 10^-5 Angstrom
Large piezoelectric coefficients indicate importance of electron-phonon coupling and low-frequency contributions to electro-optic effects
Suitable for inclusion in database of experimentally verified piezoelectric organic molecular crystals
